page: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing hyperthreading with two threads per core. The defining characteristic of this instance is its massive memory capacity of 976 GB, providing approximately 30.5 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the m3-ultramem-32 in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. The single-core stress-ng score is 1843.99, and the multi-core score is 30211.8. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1634.42 GB/sec, which drops to 146.5 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write speed of 64.54 GB/sec. Database operations, measured via PassMark, score 15229.15, while Redis SET operations achieve 30.48 million operations per second.

The server exhibits clear tradeoffs between memory capacity and compute-heavy execution. While it achieves a strong text generation speed of 278.22 tokens per second for small 135M LLM models, its performance degrades significantly on larger models, dropping to a weak 6.11 tokens per second for prompt processing and a poor 2.45 tokens per second for text generation on 70B models. This indicates a lack of optimization for large-scale AI inference without dedicated GPU acceleration. Consequently, this instance is highly suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory footprints, rather than heavy parallel computing or large-scale deep learning.
description: The Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 32 vCPUs and 976 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 30.5 GB per vCPU. Powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 16 physical cores, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a PassMark CPU score of 35890.88 and Redis SET operations reaching 30.48 million operations per second. While it handles small-scale language model text generation efficiently, performance drops significantly on larger models, such as a poor 2.45 tokens per second for 70B model text generation. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound applications, making it highly suitable for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-32 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 976 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for memory-intensive workloads and in-memory databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 976 GB RAM, and a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM capacity per vCPU, designed for large in-memory databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 976 GB of RAM delivering 30.5 GB per core
Powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Delivers 30.48 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound workloads
